aliases: Korean Mixed Rice with BBQ Pork

english: Korean Mixed Rice with BBQ Pork

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bibimbap entry (FOODON_00002572); no specific BBQ pork variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers bibimbap as a category; no BBQ pork-specific sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for bibimbap or Korean mixed rice bowl dishes.

main_ingredients: mixed vegetables (spinach, bean sprouts, carrots, zucchini, mushrooms)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of BBQ Pork Bibimbap is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of steamed rice topped with grilled marinated pork, assorted seasoned vegetables, gochujang sauce, and sesame oil.

nutrient_source.title: Bibimbap, with meat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / FAO INFOODS SEA composite estimate for Korean mixed rice bowl with pork
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for BBQ Pork Bibimbap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for bibimbap with meat (NDB reference) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Korean dish composites. Per-100g values reflect a typical bibimbap composition: steamed white rice (~50% by weight), seasoned mixed vegetables (~30%), grilled pork (~15%), and condiments including gochujang and sesame oil (~5%).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed rice dishes and Korean bibimbap analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for BBQ Pork Bibimbap. GI of 54 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed rice dishes with protein and vegetables (steamed white rice GI ~72 is moderated by the presence of protein from pork, dietary fibre from vegetables, and acidity from gochujang). The composite GI of 54 is consistent with published values for bibimbap-style mixed rice bowls in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, Diabetes Care). GL per serving = (54 × 18.5 × 4.5) / 100 = approximately 44.9 g, based on 450 g serving with 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from gochujang, soy-based marinades, and seasoned vegetables
hypertension.guidance: BBQ Pork Bibimbap contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1,395 mg per 450 g serving), which represents a significant portion of the 2,000 mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (54) and high glycemic load per serving due to white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the estimated GI of 54 is in the low range, the large carbohydrate load from white rice in a standard 450 g serving produces a glycemic load of approximately 44.9 g, which may cause postprandial glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider reducing portion size, substituting brown rice, or pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork and sodium from condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: BBQ Pork Bibimbap provides approximately 1.1 g saturated fat per 100 g and a high per-serving sodium load; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion frequency, choose leaner pork cuts, and request gochujang sauce on the side to reduce sodium and saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: BBQ Pork Bibimbap (구운 돼지고기 비빔밥; 烤猪肉拌饭) is a Korean mixed rice bowl dish consisting of steamed white rice topped with grilled or barbecued marinated pork, an assortment of seasoned vegetables (typically spinach, bean sprouts, julienned carrots, zucchini, and shiitake mushrooms), gochujang (Korean fermented chilli paste), and sesame oil. The dish is assembled in a bowl and mixed thoroughly before eating, which is the literal meaning of 'bibimbap' (비빔밥 — mixed rice). The BBQ pork variant substitutes the more common beef bulgogi or raw egg yolk with char-grilled pork that has been marinated in a soy-garlic-sesame sauce, imparting a smoky, savoury, and slightly sweet flavour profile. The texture contrasts tender pork with crisp-tender vegetables and soft rice. Nutritionally, a standard 450 g serving provides approximately 585 kcal, 29 g protein, 17 g fat, and 83 g carbohydrate. The dish is a source of iron, B vitamins, and vitamin A from the mixed vegetables. The sodium content is elevated due to gochujang and soy-based marinades, making it a moderate concern for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. The estimated glycemic index of 54 places it in the low GI category, though the glycemic load per serving is high due to the white rice base. In Singapore, this dish is served at Korean casual dining restaurants and food delivery platforms. It is suitable for non-vegetarian, non-halal diners and contains common allergens including soy, sesame, and gluten from gochujang.
common_user_queries: How many calories in BBQ pork bibimbap?
Is BBQ pork bibimbap high in sodium?
What is the GI of bibimbap?
How much protein in pork bibimbap?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for BBQ Pork Bibimbap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central bibimbap-with-meat composites and FAO/INFOODS SEA Korean dish data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed rice dishes with protein. Confidence is medium rather than low because the estimation methodology is well-grounded in approved sources and the dish composition is well-characterised.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central bibimbap composite or FAO/INFOODS SEA reference used for this record. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source composite. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible in grilled pork and vegetable bibimbap preparations per USDA SR Legacy data. The glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_100g × serving_size_g) / 100 = (54 × 18.5 × 450) / 100 / 100 = 44.9 g. This dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork. Gochujang may contain wheat-derived ingredients, making the dish unsuitable for coeliac or gluten-intolerant individuals. The field dataed in the source record (Mincheng Bibimbap 민정비빔밥 敏丁拌 — Funan, Singapore) serves this dish at SGD 9.80. Source record GD003895 from field survey data was used as the seed record only; all nutrient and GI values are derived from approved scientific sources.
